title: podman image policy docker-daemon transport
body:
### Issue Description

using **policy.json** like 
`
{
  "default": [
    {
      "type": "reject"
    }
  ],
  "transports": {
    "docker": {
      "registry.aquasec.com": [
        {
          "type": "insecureAcceptAnything"
        }
      ],
      "quay.io": [
        {
          "type": "insecureAcceptAnything"
        }
      ],
      "mcr.microsoft.com": [
        {
          "type": "insecureAcceptAnything"
        }
      ]
    },
    "docker-daemon": {
      "": [
        {
          "type": "insecureAcceptAnything"
        }
      ]
    }
  }
}
`
when I build a container and use aquascan with provisioned podman socket for API

`
docker run --rm -v /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock -v /tmp:/tmp registry.aquasec.com/scanner:2302.13.13 scan --local testimage:0.1.212 --no-verify 
`
(/var/run/docker.sock is symlink to /run/user/1001/podman/podman.sock) 

_unable to get uncompressed layer sha256:f5bb4f853c84f6579d86f72de5f1d43fc8866443d9a027e2106e3755acefd61c: failed to get the layer (sha256:f5bb4f853c84f6579d86f72de5f1d43fc8866443d9a027e2106e3755acefd61c): unable to populate: unable to open: unable to export the image: 
Error response from daemon: Source image rejected: Running image containers-storage:[overlay@/home/AzDevOps/.local/share/containers/storage+/tmp/containers-user-1001/containers]@f26ca2ef7f93332030eed7122c79c1d59bc60dcb1b237da84a11e489987ebe04 is rejected by policy._
  
**With policy.json set to default** like:
`
   {
          "default": [ { "type": "insecureAcceptAnything" } ]
    }
`
**everything is working fine.**


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. enable policy
2. run container with provisioned podman socket and run an already present container over API



### Describe the results you received

Layer usage is blocked by policy:

unable to get uncompressed layer sha256:f5bb4f853c84f6579d86f72de5f1d43fc8866443d9a027e2106e3755acefd61c: failed to get the layer (sha256:f5bb4f853c84f6579d86f72de5f1d43fc8866443d9a027e2106e3755acefd61c): unable to populate: unable to open: unable to export the image: Error response from daemon: Source image rejected: Running image containers-storage:[overlay@/home/AzDevOps/.local/share/containers/storage+/tmp/containers-user-1001/containers]@f26ca2ef7f93332030eed7122c79c1d59bc60dcb1b237da84a11e489987ebe04 is rejected by policy.

### Describe the results you expected

run as normal

### podman info output

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out, @tomasdedic.  Where (which path) did you configure the policy?  Can you pull from other registries than the aquasec one?

Cc: @mtrmac

@vrothberg policy file is in /etc/containers/policy.json

I think this is not about pulling from registries
1. build image locally, in this example image tagged as testimage:0.1.212 
2. run aquscan image with podman socket mounted and run image testimage:0.1.212 over API
`docker run --rm -v /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock -v /tmp:/tmp registry.aquasec.com/scanner:2302.13.13 scan --local testimage:0.1.212 --no-verify`
4. getting error: _Source image rejected: Running image containers-storage:[overlay@/home/AzDevOps/.local/share/containers/storage+/tmp/containers-user-1001/containers]@f26ca2ef7f93332030eed7122c79c1d59bc60dcb1b237da84a11e489987ebe04 is rejected by policy._

When I disable policy` { "default": [ { "type": "insecureAcceptAnything" } ] }` everythink is working fine.
But to response to you question yes I can pull from: registry.aquasec.com, quay.io, mcr.microsoft.com

Thanks, @tomasdedic.

Does it work when running `podman` instead of `docker`?

@vrothberg docker is alias to podman. Strictly speaking it is running
`podman run --rm -v /run/user/1001/podman/podman.sock:/var/run/docker.sock -v /tmp:/tmp registry.aquasec.com/scanner:2302.13.13 scan --local testimage:0.1.212 --no-verify`
@vrothberg there is no docker running only podman

@vrothberg I have move forward, forget about aquascan it is only accompanying phenomenon. Problem is much simplier, with policy.json defined above and simple Dockerfile

`
podman build -f Dockerfile -t 'agr1/tst:1' --log-level debug
`
_Successfully tagged localhost/agr1/tst:1
DEBU[0017] printing final image id "00fa582fae7aeca1f4c85fda4c22e7f608314ad827b83941fda57dc7033c6c46"
00fa582fae7aeca1f4c85fda4c22e7f608314ad827b83941fda57dc7033c6c46_

`
podman build -f Dockerfile -t 'agr2/tst:1' --log-level debug
`

_DEBU[0000] Using blob info cache at /var/home/core/.local/share/containers/cache/blob-info-cache-v1.boltdb
DEBU[0000] IsRunningImageAllowed for image containers-storage:[overlay@/var/home/core/.local/share/containers/storage]@00fa582fae7aeca1f4c85fda4c22e7f608314ad827b83941fda57dc7033c6c46
DEBU[0000]  Using default policy section
DEBU[0000] Requirement 0: denied, done
Error: copying image "00fa582fae7aeca1f4c85fda4c22e7f608314ad827b83941fda57dc7033c6c46": Source image rejected: Running image containers-storage:[overlay@/var/home/core/.local/share/containers/storage+/run/user/501/containers]@00fa582fae7aeca1f4c85fda4c22e7f608314ad827b83941fda57dc7033c6c46 is rejected by policy.
DEBU[0000] Shutting down engines_

**It seems that with policy above we cannot use local cache, as the policy is very poorly described in the documentation it is possible that I have missed something**

ok it seems that adding containers-storage transport works:
```json
    "transports": {
        "containers-storage": {
            "": [
                {
                    "type": "insecureAcceptAnything"
                }
            ]
        },
```

`
podman build -f Dockerfile-multistage -t 'agr2/tst:1' --log-level debug
`

_DEBU[0000] Found image "agr2/tst:1" as "localhost/agr2/tst:1" in local containers storage
DEBU[0000] Found image "agr2/tst:1" as "localhost/agr2/tst:1" in local containers storage ([overlay@/var/home/core/.local/share/containers/storage+/run/user/501/containers]@00fa582fae7aeca1f4c85fda4c22e7f608314ad827b83941fda57dc7033c6c46)
DEBU[0000] exporting opaque data as blob "sha256:00fa582fae7aeca1f4c85fda4c22e7f608314ad827b83941fda57dc7033c6c46"
DEBU[0000] parsed reference into "[overlay@/var/home/core/.local/share/containers/storage+/run/user/501/containers]localhost/agr2/tst:1"
Successfully tagged localhost/agr2/tst:1
Successfully tagged localhost/agr1/tst:1
DEBU[0000] printing final image id "00fa582fae7aeca1f4c85fda4c22e7f608314ad827b83941fda57dc7033c6c46"
00fa582fae7aeca1f4c85fda4c22e7f608314ad827b83941fda57dc7033c6c46_

```sh
podman images
REPOSITORY                    TAG         IMAGE ID      CREATED         SIZE
localhost/agr2/tst            1           00fa582fae7a  13 minutes ago  75.2 MB
localhost/agr1/tst            1           00fa582fae7a  13 minutes ago  75.2 MB
```
Nice catch, @tomasdedic.  I am glad you got it working!

Returning to this late, I’m afraid:

IIRC (and looking at libimage’s `PolicyAllowStorage` and copies of `storageAllowedPolicyScopes` in Buildah seems to confirm) that Buildah intends, at least for `commit` and `push`, to automatically override `policy.json`.

I’m not sure that we should _extend_ that too far (I recognize the pragmatic value of that override, but to a point…) but if a plain `podman build` fails, is that a bug in the code as currently intended? Or is it one of the operations that we did not intend to override?

---

In particular, if the “unable to export the image” refers to something like `podman save`, I’m not surprised that it fails with a restrictive policy.

I am a bit more surprised that `podman build` fails — AFAICS it’s a fairly thin wrapper against Buildah. (But it could still very well be the case that this is currently intended behavior.)

---

As far as a recommendation to users … yes, setting the policy for `containers-storage` to `insecureAcceptAnything` is a good idea on a local development box, to allow creating/exporting/… unsigned images. On other systems, e.g. on cluster nodes that are intended to only run images and do nothing else, the `reject` behavior could well be more appropriate.
